title: Grand Atrium Gallery-Tour Contrast Matrix
strategies: arrow baseline: all +1 with -1 on the sub-diagonal, |det|=2^(N-1) (the minimum non-zero determinant, which the checker uses as its baseline B)
seeded multi-restart random search: draw many random +/-1 matrices, keep the one with the largest determinant (compared with a fast float log-det); already far above the arrow baseline
float-guided local hill climbing: start from the best random array and repeatedly apply the single sign flip that most increases |det|, using an exact float log-determinant to rank flips
structured optimum: Sylvester-Hadamard for power-of-two N and Paley type-I (Legendre-symbol Jacobsthal) for N=q+1 with q prime == 3 mod 4, attaining |det|=N^(N/2) exactly
